title: quercus × nipponica var. koreana
intro: Quercus × nipponica var. koreana is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Fagaceae family, known for its unique characteristics and medicinal properties. It is a hybrid of Quercus acutissima and Quercus mongolica, and is also known as Korean corkbark oak or Korean white oak. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Quercus × nipponica var. koreana include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues, while also providing antioxidant and anti-aging benefits.
